cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The question is about what the films children watch at school actually do according to the text. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to find the relevant part of the text. The passage mentions that children "view motion pictures which are supposedly meant to teach them how to deal with arms safely but which are actually designed to stimulate a desire to own a gun." So the key here is that the films are supposed to teach safety but actually aim to encourage wanting a gun. Looking at the options, option D says "encourage them to have guns of their own," which matches the part about "stimulate a desire to own a gun." Option A says they teach safely handling guns, which is what the films are supposed to do, but the actual purpose is different. The question is about what the films actually do, not what they claim to do. So A is not the right answer.
